programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: Discover why it takes a village to raise lion cubs in Kenya and sniff out the mating strategy of fragrant lemurs in Madagascar. Cut a rug at a honeybee dance party, then listen up as a forked-tailed drongo tricks other birds out of their dinner.

credits.director: Kirstie McLure: Producer, Kait Borsay: Narrator

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Feel heartbreak when Dr. Amanda’s team loses a pangolin abused by wildlife traffickers, but cheer when another is released post-rehab. Check on a trio of hedgehogs and tag along as the center’s baboons and nyala antelopes get a change of scenery.

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Strap in for Dr. Amanda’s risky attempt to collar a wild roan antelope. Later, chase down blue monkey Indigo while unrest ensues among the vervets. Examine an eagle owl with bumblefoot and say adieu to Sheila the croc, who’s headed back to the wild.

programme.title: Zambia Untamed
programme.desc: Mvula is the youngest calf in the Madzi family. Temperatures are soaring. Lagoons and waterholes are drying up. This forces Mvula and his family to undertake a long and dangerous journey to the only remaining source of water: the Luangwa River.

credits.director: Martha Holmes, James Smith, Kate Beetham, Hannah Hoare:Producers

programme.title: Mysteries of the Mekong
programme.desc: The final country on the Mekong’s journey is the home of the rivers mighty delta. A large deposit of rich nutrients carried downstream from hundreds of kilometres makes this region, the jewel of Vietnam’s crown, a biological treasure trove.

programme.title: Macaque Island S1E1 -The Young Up-Start
programme.desc: Explore the lush treed cityscape where fluffy male Matnoor rules over the 30-member-strong Street Troop with an iron fist. Then meet Aarav, the old alpha who oversees the wilder Hill Troop. Which will up-and-comer Loci challenge for the crown.

credits.director: Producers: Donovan Chan, Jocelyn Little; Director: Claire Clements

programme.title: Macaque Island
programme.desc: Go on a junk-food-fuelled urban holiday with the Street Troop, then watch the Hill Troop’s invasion of their turf turn sour. Monitor the Sisterhood’s plot to kidnap Sara’s baby before newly minted alpha Loci discovers his old rival is back in town.

credits.director: Producers: Donovan Chan, Jocelyn Little; Director: Claire Clements

programme.title: Dawn to Dusk
programme.desc: Celebrate the first day of spring with Aussie creatures looking for either a dinner or a date. From the kangaroos in the grasslands to the koalas in the trees to the freshwater crocodiles, they hunt for food or fight for breeding rights.

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Feel heartbreak when Dr. Amanda’s team loses a pangolin abused by wildlife traffickers, but cheer when another is released post-rehab. Check on a trio of hedgehogs and tag along as the center’s baboons and nyala antelopes get a change of scenery.

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Strap in for Dr. Amanda’s risky attempt to collar a wild roan antelope. Later, chase down blue monkey Indigo while unrest ensues among the vervets. Examine an eagle owl with bumblefoot and say adieu to Sheila the croc, who’s headed back to the wild.

programme.title: Zambia Untamed
programme.desc: Mvula is the youngest calf in the Madzi family. Temperatures are soaring. Lagoons and waterholes are drying up. This forces Mvula and his family to undertake a long and dangerous journey to the only remaining source of water: the Luangwa River.

credits.director: Martha Holmes, James Smith, Kate Beetham, Hannah Hoare:Producers

programme.title: Mysteries of the Mekong
programme.desc: The final country on the Mekong’s journey is the home of the rivers mighty delta. A large deposit of rich nutrients carried downstream from hundreds of kilometres makes this region, the jewel of Vietnam’s crown, a biological treasure trove.

programme.title: Macaque Island S1E1 -The Young Up-Start
programme.desc: Explore the lush treed cityscape where fluffy male Matnoor rules over the 30-member-strong Street Troop with an iron fist. Then meet Aarav, the old alpha who oversees the wilder Hill Troop. Which will up-and-comer Loci challenge for the crown.

credits.director: Producers: Donovan Chan, Jocelyn Little; Director: Claire Clements

programme.title: Macaque Island
programme.desc: Go on a junk-food-fuelled urban holiday with the Street Troop, then watch the Hill Troop’s invasion of their turf turn sour. Monitor the Sisterhood’s plot to kidnap Sara’s baby before newly minted alpha Loci discovers his old rival is back in town.

credits.director: Producers: Donovan Chan, Jocelyn Little; Director: Claire Clements

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: Discover why it takes a village to raise lion cubs in Kenya and sniff out the mating strategy of fragrant lemurs in Madagascar. Cut a rug at a honeybee dance party, then listen up as a forked-tailed drongo tricks other birds out of their dinner.

credits.director: Kirstie McLure: Producer, Kait Borsay: Narrator

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Feel heartbreak when Dr. Amanda’s team loses a pangolin abused by wildlife traffickers, but cheer when another is released post-rehab. Check on a trio of hedgehogs and tag along as the center’s baboons and nyala antelopes get a change of scenery.

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Strap in for Dr. Amanda’s risky attempt to collar a wild roan antelope. Later, chase down blue monkey Indigo while unrest ensues among the vervets. Examine an eagle owl with bumblefoot and say adieu to Sheila the croc, who’s headed back to the wild.

programme.title: City Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Join the rescue team as they respond to a call about an opossum stuck in a fence that turns out not to be the kind of animal that plays dead. Inside the center, a goose with a serious foot injury receives physiotherapy before returning to the wild.

credits.director: Stacey Hatch, David Hatch, Sharone Ostrovsky:Producers

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Feel heartbreak when Dr. Amanda’s team loses a pangolin abused by wildlife traffickers, but cheer when another is released post-rehab. Check on a trio of hedgehogs and tag along as the center’s baboons and nyala antelopes get a change of scenery.

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Strap in for Dr. Amanda’s risky attempt to collar a wild roan antelope. Later, chase down blue monkey Indigo while unrest ensues among the vervets. Examine an eagle owl with bumblefoot and say adieu to Sheila the croc, who’s headed back to the wild.

programme.title: Dawn to Dusk
programme.desc: Meet the unique primates and reptiles searching for water and dodging predators, from ring-tailed lemurs in the east coast rainforests to tiny brown leaf chameleons, in this land of extreme climate variations and habitats.

programme.title: Humanlike: The Chimps of Fongoli
programme.desc: Join anthropologist Jill Pruetz and guide Dondo (Johnny) Kanté and watch evolution happen in real time. Meet the elusive chimps of Fongoli, Senegal, who use tools, interact with fire, and adapt to the human world as it collides with their own.

credits.director: Oliver Twinch: Director, David Allen, Oliver Twinch: Producers

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: Travel to the ends of the Earth, where a drama is unfolding in real time. Meet animals, from tiny butterflies to huge humpback whales, that are acting as sentinels, adapting their behaviour, and helping us unlock the riddles of our changing planet.

credits.director: Shane Campbell-Stanton: Narrator; David Allen, Oliver Twitch: Producers

programme.title: Baboons: Rules of the Troop
programme.desc: Follow four families of four different species - the resourceful Beach Troop, the warlike Desert Troop, the disciplined River Troop, and the peaceful Mountain Band - to find out why these primates are among the most successful animals in Africa.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Mekong
programme.desc: The Mekong Delta is one of the largest on Earth. Formed over the last 8000 years, it fans out and discharges into the South China Sea via nine channels. These channels split and shape the coastline, creating unique habitats for a variety of animals.

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Feel heartbreak when Dr. Amanda’s team loses a pangolin abused by wildlife traffickers, but cheer when another is released post-rehab. Check on a trio of hedgehogs and tag along as the center’s baboons and nyala antelopes get a change of scenery.

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Strap in for Dr. Amanda’s risky attempt to collar a wild roan antelope. Later, chase down blue monkey Indigo while unrest ensues among the vervets. Examine an eagle owl with bumblefoot and say adieu to Sheila the croc, who’s headed back to the wild.

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: Marvel at the toothy fish that help sharks maintain their pearly whites and discover the signature calls of dolphin families. Examine capuchin monkey’s nut-cracking stone tools, get the warm fuzzies when giraffes snuggle, and meet a meerkat mobster.

credits.director: Kirstie McLure: Producer, Kait Borsay: Narrator

programme.title: Humanlike: The Chimps of Fongoli
programme.desc: Join anthropologist Jill Pruetz and guide Dondo (Johnny) Kanté and watch evolution happen in real time. Meet the elusive chimps of Fongoli, Senegal, who use tools, interact with fire, and adapt to the human world as it collides with their own.

credits.director: Oliver Twinch: Director, David Allen, Oliver Twinch: Producers

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: Travel to the ends of the Earth, where a drama is unfolding in real time. Meet animals, from tiny butterflies to huge humpback whales, that are acting as sentinels, adapting their behaviour, and helping us unlock the riddles of our changing planet.

credits.director: Shane Campbell-Stanton: Narrator; David Allen, Oliver Twitch: Producers

programme.title: Baboons: Rules of the Troop
programme.desc: Follow four families of four different species - the resourceful Beach Troop, the warlike Desert Troop, the disciplined River Troop, and the peaceful Mountain Band - to find out why these primates are among the most successful animals in Africa.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Mekong
programme.desc: The Mekong Delta is one of the largest on Earth. Formed over the last 8000 years, it fans out and discharges into the South China Sea via nine channels. These channels split and shape the coastline, creating unique habitats for a variety of animals.

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Feel heartbreak when Dr. Amanda’s team loses a pangolin abused by wildlife traffickers, but cheer when another is released post-rehab. Check on a trio of hedgehogs and tag along as the center’s baboons and nyala antelopes get a change of scenery.

programme.title: Shadowlands
programme.desc: Learn how winter’s arrival triggers animal survival instincts in southwestern New Zealand. Track dolphins as they move to warmer waters to give their calves a fighting chance, and join longfin eels on an epic migration to their spawning grounds.

credits.director: Judith Curran: Developed By, Lemuel Lyes: Senior Archive Producer

programme.title: Dawn to Dusk
programme.desc: Meet the unique primates and reptiles searching for water and dodging predators, from ring-tailed lemurs in the east coast rainforests to tiny brown leaf chameleons, in this land of extreme climate variations and habitats.

programme.title: Humanlike: The Chimps of Fongoli
programme.desc: Join anthropologist Jill Pruetz and guide Dondo (Johnny) Kanté and watch evolution happen in real time. Meet the elusive chimps of Fongoli, Senegal, who use tools, interact with fire, and adapt to the human world as it collides with their own.

credits.director: Oliver Twinch: Director, David Allen, Oliver Twinch: Producers

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: Travel to the ends of the Earth, where a drama is unfolding in real time. Meet animals, from tiny butterflies to huge humpback whales, that are acting as sentinels, adapting their behaviour, and helping us unlock the riddles of our changing planet.

credits.director: Shane Campbell-Stanton: Narrator; David Allen, Oliver Twitch: Producers

programme.title: Baboons: Rules of the Troop
programme.desc: Follow four families of four different species - the resourceful Beach Troop, the warlike Desert Troop, the disciplined River Troop, and the peaceful Mountain Band - to find out why these primates are among the most successful animals in Africa.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Mekong
programme.desc: The Mekong Delta is one of the largest on Earth. Formed over the last 8000 years, it fans out and discharges into the South China Sea via nine channels. These channels split and shape the coastline, creating unique habitats for a variety of animals.

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Feel heartbreak when Dr. Amanda’s team loses a pangolin abused by wildlife traffickers, but cheer when another is released post-rehab. Check on a trio of hedgehogs and tag along as the center’s baboons and nyala antelopes get a change of scenery.

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Strap in for Dr. Amanda’s risky attempt to collar a wild roan antelope. Later, chase down blue monkey Indigo while unrest ensues among the vervets. Examine an eagle owl with bumblefoot and say adieu to Sheila the croc, who’s headed back to the wild.
